description: Comprehensive code quality improvements for the Chrome extension: extract shared constants, reduce duplication, improve modularity, add missing unit tests, and fix bugs. Makes the codebase cleaner, more testable, and easier to maintain.

userStories.title: Define JSON-RPC error code constants and replace all magic numbers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want JSON-RPC error codes defined as named constants so that code is self-documenting and error-prone copy-paste of numeric literals is eliminated.
acceptanceCriteria: A new file platform/browser-extension/src/json-rpc-errors.ts exports named constants: JSONRPC_METHOD_NOT_FOUND (-32601), JSONRPC_INVALID_PARAMS (-32602), JSONRPC_INTERNAL_ERROR (-32603), JSONRPC_NO_USABLE_TAB (-32001), JSONRPC_ADAPTER_NOT_READY (-32002)
Every raw numeric error code literal (-32001, -32002, -32601, -32602, -32603) across all .ts files in platform/browser-extension/src/ is replaced with the corresponding named constant
No raw -32xxx numeric literals remain in any source file (test files may still use raw numbers)
userStories.notes: There are ~58 occurrences across 12 files. Key files: dispatch-helpers.ts, tool-dispatch.ts, resource-prompt-dispatch.ts, message-router.ts, browser-commands/helpers.ts, browser-commands/interaction-commands.ts, browser-commands/extension-commands.ts, browser-commands/tab-commands.ts, browser-commands/cookie-commands.ts, browser-commands/content-commands.ts, browser-commands/resource-commands.ts. Also consider adding a sendJsonRpcError(id, code, message) helper to reduce the inline { jsonrpc: '2.0', error: { code, message }, id } construction pattern. The file should be placed alongside constants.ts.

userStories.title: Extract shared constants for magic numbers across the extension
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all repeated magic numbers extracted to named constants in constants.ts so that they are discoverable and maintainable in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/browser-extension/src/constants.ts includes: SERVER_PORT_KEY ('serverPort'), DEFAULT_SERVER_PORT (9515), TEXT_PREVIEW_MAX_LENGTH (200), DEFAULT_WAIT_TIMEOUT_MS (10000), POLL_INTERVAL_MS (100), DEFAULT_QUERY_LIMIT (100), MAX_INPUT_SIZE (10 * 1024 * 1024), SIDE_PANEL_TIMEOUT_MS (3000), CDP_VERSION ('1.3'), WS_CLOSE_PONG_TIMEOUT (4000), WS_CLOSE_AUTH_FAILED (4001), WS_INFO_TIMEOUT_MS (3000), EXEC_MAX_ASYNC_WAIT_MS (10000), EXEC_POLL_INTERVAL_MS (50), EXEC_RESULT_TRUNCATION_LIMIT (50000), DEFAULT_LOG_LIMIT (100)
All corresponding magic numbers/strings in source files are replaced with the imported constants
The string 'serverPort' no longer appears as a raw literal in background.ts or offscreen/index.ts
userStories.notes: The magic number 200 (text preview truncation) appears 5x in interaction-commands.ts (lines 32, 207, 258, 477, 587). The string 'serverPort' appears in background.ts lines 176, 348. The CDP version '1.3' appears in network-capture.ts and resource-commands.ts. The WS close codes 4000/4001 appear in offscreen/index.ts. The 3_000 timeout for /ws-info fetch appears 4x in offscreen/index.ts. MAX_INPUT_SIZE is a local const in tool-dispatch.ts:347. DEFAULT_LOG_LIMIT=100 is in extension-commands.ts:112. SIDE_PANEL_TIMEOUT_MS is local in extension-commands.ts:130. EXEC_MAX_ASYNC_WAIT_MS/EXEC_POLL_INTERVAL_MS/EXEC_RESULT_TRUNCATION_LIMIT are magic numbers in extension-commands.ts:365,365,392.

userStories.title: Extract tab.stateChanged payload builder and deduplicate notification dispatch
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single factory function for tab.stateChanged payloads so that the 5 construction sites across 2 files are consolidated.
acceptanceCriteria: A named interface PluginTabStateInfo { state: TabState; tabId: number | null; url: string | null } is defined and used wherever this shape appears (tab-state.ts, message-router.ts)
A helper function sendTabStateNotification(pluginName, stateInfo) encapsulates both sendToServer and forwardToSidePanel calls with the correct JSON-RPC shape
tab-state.ts and message-router.ts both use the shared helper instead of constructing payloads inline
The tab.stateChanged payload is no longer constructed in more than 2 places (the helper definition + possibly sendTabSyncAll which has a different shape)
userStories.notes: Currently constructed at: tab-state.ts:142-149 (side panel only after syncAll), tab-state.ts:270-278 (sendToServer), tab-state.ts:281-293 (forwardToSidePanel), message-router.ts:371-380 (sendToServer), message-router.ts:382-394 (forwardToSidePanel). The helper could live in messaging.ts or a new tab-state-notifications.ts. The PluginTabStateInfo interface should go in extension-messages.ts alongside other shared types.

userStories.title: Extract confirmation badge/notification logic from message-router.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the confirmation badge/notification management extracted into its own module so that message-router.ts focuses solely on routing.
acceptanceCriteria: A new file platform/browser-extension/src/confirmation-badge.ts exports: notifyConfirmationRequest, clearConfirmationBadge, clearAllConfirmationBadges, and an init() function that registers the chrome.notifications.onClicked listener
message-router.ts no longer contains pendingConfirmationCount, updateConfirmationBadge, or chrome.notifications logic
background.ts calls the init() function from confirmation-badge.ts at startup
The chrome.notifications.onClicked listener is no longer a side effect of importing message-router.ts
userStories.notes: The code to extract is in message-router.ts lines ~490-551: pendingConfirmationCount variable, updateConfirmationBadge function, notifyConfirmationRequest function, clearConfirmationBadge function, clearAllConfirmationBadges function, and the chrome.notifications.onClicked.addListener block. The listener registration at module load time is a side effect that causes issues in tests (known-methods.test.ts has to stub chrome.notifications.onClicked just because it imports methodHandlerNames). Moving it behind an init() function fixes this.

userStories.title: Add wrapSync error boundary and standardize async handler patterns in message-router.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want consistent error handling across all dispatch table entries so that sync handler errors are caught and async handlers use a uniform wrapper.
acceptanceCriteria: wrapSync catches errors and logs them with the method name, matching wrapAsync's error handling pattern
sync.full, plugin.update, and plugin.uninstall handlers use wrapAsync or a new wrapNotification wrapper instead of ad-hoc inline error handling
The extension.reload handler is extracted to a named function (not inlined in the dispatch table)
handlePluginUninstall wraps cleanupAdaptersInMatchingTabs in try-catch (consistent with handleSyncFull using Promise.allSettled for the same operation)
All dispatch table entries use the same wrapper patterns consistently
userStories.notes: Current issues: wrapSync (lines 61-65) has no try-catch — if a sync handler throws, the error is unhandled. sync.full and plugin.update (lines 213-226) use manual inline .catch() instead of wrapAsync because they are notifications (no id required). Create a wrapNotification helper that always executes (no id guard). plugin.uninstall (line 228-234) could use wrapAsync. The extension.reload handler (lines 189-211) is 20+ lines inlined in the dispatch table. handlePluginUninstall line 428 calls cleanupAdaptersInMatchingTabs without try-catch — if it throws, removePlugin and clearPluginTabState never run.

userStories.title: Extract background.ts onMessage handler into a dispatch map with testable handlers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the background.ts chrome.runtime.onMessage handler refactored from a monolithic 156-line switch into a dispatch map with individually testable handler functions.
acceptanceCriteria: The onMessage switch statement in background.ts is replaced with a Map-based dispatch or a module with named handler functions
Each message type handler is a named, exported function that can be unit-tested independently
The side panel toggle logic (openWindows state, onOpened/onClosed, action.onClicked) is extracted to a separate module (e.g., side-panel-toggle.ts)
The 'serverPort' string is replaced with the SERVER_PORT_KEY constant from US-002
The ws://localhost:${port}/ws URL template is a shared utility function (not duplicated)
content-script-originated messages (plugin:logs, tool:progress) validate their payload at runtime (Array.isArray check for entries, type checks for progress fields)
userStories.notes: The switch statement spans background.ts lines 156-312. Side panel toggle logic is lines 10-33 (completely independent concern with its own openWindows state). The ws URL template appears at lines 179 and 349. Missing runtime validation: plugin:logs handler (line 235) iterates message.entries without checking Array.isArray — content scripts can send arbitrary data. tool:progress handler (lines 253-270) trusts dispatchId/progress/total/message without validation. Return true is unnecessary for synchronous handlers (ws:message, bg:send, bg:getConnectionState, plugin:logs, tool:progress, sp:confirmationResponse, port-changed) — only offscreen:getUrl needs it.

userStories.title: Deduplicate tab collection and teardown logic in iife-injection.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the duplicated tab collection and adapter teardown code in iife-injection.ts extracted into shared helpers to reduce maintenance burden.
acceptanceCriteria: A shared helper function queryMatchingTabs(urlPatterns: string[]) encapsulates the tab querying and deduplication logic that was previously duplicated
injectPluginIntoMatchingTabs and cleanupAdaptersInMatchingTabs both use the shared helper
The adapter teardown logic (calling teardown() on the __openTabs global) is consolidated into a single function used by both injection and cleanup
The tabMap that previously stored full chrome.tabs.Tab objects but only used keys is changed to track only tab IDs where appropriate
injectLogRelay logs a warning on failure instead of silently swallowing errors
verifyAdapterVersion returns its result so the caller can decide how to handle mismatches
userStories.notes: Tab collection is duplicated at iife-injection.ts lines 254-266 and 379-392 — nearly character-for-character identical. Teardown logic duplicated at lines 279-302 (injectPluginIntoMatchingTabs) and 398-431 (cleanupAdaptersInMatchingTabs). The tabMap (Map<number, chrome.tabs.Tab>) only uses .keys() — a Set<number> or just the tab IDs would be more precise. injectLogRelay line 161-163 has a silent catch {} that should at minimum console.warn. verifyAdapterVersion (lines 38-61) only logs warnings but never returns success/failure to the caller.

userStories.title: Refactor offscreen/index.ts: extract duplicated patterns and reduce module-level state
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the offscreen WebSocket module refactored to extract duplicated patterns (ws-info fetch, reconnect logic, URL conversion) and reduce the number of module-level mutable variables.
acceptanceCriteria: A fetchWsInfo(httpBase, secret) helper encapsulates the /ws-info fetch + 401 retry pattern (previously duplicated in refreshWsUrl and ws:setUrl handler)
A wsToHttpBase(wsUrl) helper encapsulates the ws->http URL conversion (previously duplicated at lines 200 and 403)
A reconnectWithNewUrl(url) or disconnectAndReconnect() helper encapsulates the close-or-cancel-and-reconnect pattern (previously duplicated 3x in ws:setUrl, port-changed, bg:forceReconnect)
Named constants WS_CLOSE_PONG_TIMEOUT and WS_CLOSE_AUTH_FAILED replace magic close codes 4000 and 4001
The silent JSON.parse catch in onmessage (line ~312) logs a console.warn for debuggability
The pong timeout correctly uses a semantically appropriate DisconnectReason (not 'connection_refused' for a zombie connection)
userStories.notes: The /ws-info fetch + 401 retry pattern appears at offscreen/index.ts lines 205-217 (refreshWsUrl) and lines 410-425 (ws:setUrl handler). The ws->http URL conversion appears at lines 200 and 403. The reconnect pattern appears 3x: ws:setUrl (lines 446-466), port-changed (lines 506-518), bg:forceReconnect (lines 482-498). Close code 4000 is at line 148, 4001 at line 332. The silent JSON.parse catch is at line 312. The pong timeout at line 154 sets lastDisconnectReason to 'connection_refused' which is semantically wrong — it's a timeout, not a connection refusal. If the DisconnectReason type in extension-messages.ts needs a new variant (e.g., 'timeout'), add it there. The WS_INFO_TIMEOUT_MS constant from US-002 should replace the 4x 3_000 literal.

userStories.title: Split interaction-commands.ts and fix the visibility check bug
userStories.description: As a developer, I want interaction-commands.ts split into smaller files and the visibility check logical bug fixed so that the code is maintainable and correct.
acceptanceCriteria: handleBrowserPressKey and handleBrowserScroll are extracted into separate files (e.g., key-press-command.ts, scroll-command.ts) or the file is reorganized into logical groups
The visibility check in handleBrowserWaitForElement is fixed: the || (OR) between offsetParent and display checks is changed to && (AND) so both conditions must be true for visibility
The magic number 200 (text preview truncation) uses the TEXT_PREVIEW_MAX_LENGTH constant from US-002
Parameter validation for required string params (text, key, action, direction) uses a consistent helper pattern (like requireStringParam) instead of inline sendToServer calls
The scroll handler validates the direction parameter against known values (up, down, left, right) and returns an error for invalid values instead of silently doing nothing
userStories.notes: The visibility check bug is at interaction-commands.ts line 201: `const isVisible = !vis || htmlEl.offsetParent !== null || getComputedStyle(htmlEl).display !== 'none';` — the || should be && because both conditions must hold for true visibility. The file is 650 lines with 9 handlers. handleBrowserPressKey (lines 274-421, 148 lines) and handleBrowserScroll (lines 423-544, 122 lines) are the largest and most complex. Direction validation is missing — an invalid direction causes the scroll func to silently do nothing (dx=0, dy=0). The inline parameter validation at lines 58-61, 278-282, 127-134, 614-622 should use requireStringParam. Consider also adding a requireStringParam to browser-commands/helpers.ts alongside requireTabId/requireSelector/requireUrl.

userStories.title: Fix tab-state.ts race condition and extract affected-plugins logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the race condition in tab-state.ts fixed (updateLastKnownState bypasses pluginLocks) and the affected-plugins filtering extracted for testability.
acceptanceCriteria: updateLastKnownState either goes through the pluginLocks serialization mechanism or the concurrency contract is explicitly documented and safe
The duplicate error logging in checkTabStateChanges (both the .catch on pluginLocks and the Promise.allSettled rejection handler) is consolidated into a single error-handling path
The boolean removed parameter in checkTabStateChanges is replaced with two separate functions (checkTabRemoved and checkTabChanged) with distinct signatures
The ad-hoc type { url?: string; status?: string } for changeInfo is replaced with chrome.tabs.TabChangeInfo
The anonymous inline return type { state: TabState; tabId: number | null; url: string | null } is a named interface (PluginTabStateInfo from US-003)
userStories.notes: Race condition: updateLastKnownState (line 179, called from message-router.ts handlePluginUpdate) writes to lastKnownState without going through pluginLocks. checkTabStateChanges (line 258-301) uses pluginLocks to serialize access. If handlePluginUpdate and checkTabStateChanges run concurrently for the same plugin, lastKnownState can become inconsistent. Fix options: (a) route updateLastKnownState through the lock, (b) use computeAndNotifyStateChange from tab-state.ts in message-router.ts instead of direct access. Duplicate error logging: line 297 .catch logs plugin-specific errors, line 304-308 Promise.allSettled also logs rejections — same error logged twice. The boolean removed flag changes behavior fundamentally — true skips all URL-based filtering. Two separate functions would be clearer.

userStories.title: Add requireStringParam helper and standardize parameter validation across browser commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a requireStringParam helper in browser-commands/helpers.ts so that required string parameter validation is consistent across all command handlers.
acceptanceCriteria: browser-commands/helpers.ts exports a requireStringParam(params, paramName, id) function that validates the param is a non-empty string and sends a JSON-RPC error if not, returning null on failure (matching the requireTabId/requireSelector pattern)

All inline string parameter validations in interaction-commands.ts (text, key, action, direction, value/label), extension-commands.ts (plugin), resource-commands.ts (url), and other command files are replaced with requireStringParam
helpers.test.ts includes tests for requireStringParam covering: missing param, wrong type, empty string, valid string
userStories.notes: Currently, string param validation is done ad-hoc in many command files with inline sendToServer({ jsonrpc, error: { code: -32602, message }, id }) calls. Examples: interaction-commands.ts lines 58-61 (text), 278-282 (key), 127-134 (value/label), 614-622 (action). extension-commands.ts lines 159-167 (plugin), 340-347 (filePath). resource-commands.ts lines 146-149 (url). The helper should follow the exact same pattern as requireTabId (return the validated value or null, send error as side effect). Use the JSONRPC_INVALID_PARAMS constant from US-001.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for dispatch pipeline: tool-dispatch.ts and dispatch-helpers extensions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for the tool dispatch pipeline so that the most critical untested code path in the extension has regression coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: A new test file platform/browser-extension/src/tool-dispatch.test.ts exists with tests covering: notifyDispatchProgress (Map lookup, callback invocation, missing key), handleToolDispatch parameter validation (missing plugin/tool/input, invalid input type, oversized input), getPluginLink (npm package, scoped package, local/dev plugin)
Tests mock chrome.scripting.executeScript and chrome.runtime.sendMessage to verify the dispatch orchestration
Test coverage includes the progress callback registration and timeout reset flow
All tests pass with bun run test
userStories.notes: tool-dispatch.ts has zero tests despite being 396 lines of critical dispatch logic. The most easily testable functions: notifyDispatchProgress (line 19-22, Map lookup), getPluginLink (lines 27-35, pure function with 3 branches), handleToolDispatch parameter validation (lines 326-372). The progress flow test should verify: register callback via executeToolOnTab mock → call notifyDispatchProgress → verify the timeout was cleared and reset. Mock chrome.scripting.executeScript to return controlled results. Use the same chrome mock patterns established in message-router.test.ts and dispatch-helpers.test.ts.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for iife-injection.ts pure logic and tab-state.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for the extractable pure logic in iife-injection.ts and tab-state.ts so that the injection and tab state modules have regression coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: A new test file platform/browser-extension/src/iife-injection.test.ts exists testing: queryMatchingTabs (from US-007 extraction), isSafePluginName validation (exported for testing), the adapter presence/version/hash verification flow with mocked chrome.scripting

A new test file platform/browser-extension/src/tab-state.test.ts exists testing: computePluginTabState with mocked chrome.scripting, lastKnownState cache behavior (update, clear, get), checkTabRemoved/checkTabChanged (from US-010 extraction) with mocked dependencies
All tests pass with bun run test
userStories.notes: iife-injection.ts has zero tests. After US-007's refactoring, the extracted queryMatchingTabs and teardown helpers are independently testable. isSafePluginName (line 9) should be exported. For tab-state.ts, after US-010's refactoring with split functions, tests can verify: computePluginTabState returns correct state for ready/unavailable/closed cases with mocked chrome.scripting, lastKnownState cache correctly tracks and diffs states, checkTabRemoved iterates all plugins, checkTabChanged filters by URL patterns. Use the same chrome mock setup as plugin-storage.test.ts and dispatch-helpers.test.ts.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for browser command handlers: parameter validation paths
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for parameter validation in browser command handlers so that invalid input is reliably rejected with proper error responses.
acceptanceCriteria: New test files exist for at least: interaction-commands.test.ts, extension-commands.test.ts, tab-commands.test.ts covering their parameter validation paths
Each test verifies that invalid params (missing tabId, missing selector, invalid types) result in the correct JSON-RPC error response via sendToServer
Tests for handleBrowserPressKey verify the key validation path
Tests for handleBrowserScroll verify direction validation (from US-009)
Tests for handleExtensionCheckAdapter verify plugin name validation
Tests for handleBrowserExecuteScript verify filePath validation
All tests pass with bun run test
userStories.notes: The entire browser-commands/ directory (6 command files) has zero tests except helpers.test.ts. Focus on parameter validation paths since those are testable without complex chrome.scripting mocks. For each handler, test: missing tabId, missing required params (selector, text, key, plugin, filePath, etc.), invalid param types. Use the same mock pattern as helpers.test.ts which stubs chrome.runtime.id and sendToServer. After US-011 adds requireStringParam, the validation paths should be more uniform and easier to test.

userStories.title: Standardize sendSuccessResult/sendErrorResult usage across all browser commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I want consistent use of sendSuccessResult and sendErrorResult across all browser command handlers so that response construction is uniform and the code is easier to audit.
acceptanceCriteria: handleExtensionGetSidePanel uses sendSuccessResult for all response paths instead of raw sendToServer
handleExtensionCheckAdapter uses sendErrorResult for validation error paths instead of raw sendToServer
handleBrowserExecuteScript uses sendErrorResult for validation error paths instead of raw sendToServer
All browser command handler files use only sendSuccessResult/sendErrorResult (no raw sendToServer calls for tool/browser command responses)
handleBrowserScroll explicitly picks result properties in sendSuccessResult (consistent with all other handlers, fixing the inconsistency where it passes raw result)
userStories.notes: handleExtensionGetSidePanel (extension-commands.ts lines 138, 143, 150) uses raw sendToServer for all responses. handleExtensionCheckAdapter (lines 161-166, 171-176) uses raw sendToServer for validation errors. handleBrowserExecuteScript (lines 341, 345) uses raw sendToServer for validation errors. handleBrowserScroll (interaction-commands.ts line 540) passes raw result to sendSuccessResult instead of explicitly picking properties like every other handler does. This is the final story so it includes e2eCheckpoint: true to run the full verification suite before merge.
